matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: KITLG pigmentation variation model (KITLG hair pigmentation and graying pathway model)
KITLG regulatory expression context model (KITLG expression and methylation-context model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a KITLG hair pigmentation and graying thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_prediction_rules.common_name: KITLG hair pigmentation and graying pathway model
variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed KITLG pigmentation variation model dosage suggests this sample carries the KITLG prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a KITLG hair pigmentation and graying thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. This specific marker is curated as KITLG hair pigmentation and graying pathway model. KITLG pigmentation variation model is curated as a KITLG marker for Hair pigmentation, skin pigmentation, and graying research context, Melanocyte development, survival, and melanosome biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched KITLG pigmentation variation model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the KITLG prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a KITLG hair pigmentation and graying thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. An observed KITLG variant places this sample in the curated KITLG research context. The local KITLG bundle is oriented toward hair pigmentation and graying. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation. KITLG variants can alter, tag, or contextualize melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a KITLG variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a KITLG hair pigmentation and graying thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. KITLG methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
